title: Model each lap as a set of cars with a permutation-invariant field-context network
summary: Pit timing depends on local field state, not just a driver's own tyre age: traffic, peer tyre ages, and who else looks pit-ready matter.
rationale: Pit timing depends on local field state, not just a driver's own tyre age: traffic, peer tyre ages, and who else looks pit-ready matter. Deep Sets and Set Transformer are built for permutation-invariant set inputs and are a better fit than manual lap aggregates for race-lap context.
implementation_hint: For each Year-Race-LapNumber, build a set of all active cars using current numeric features and compound embeddings; train a small Deep Sets or Set Transformer teacher to output per-car pit probability, then use its OOF prediction as a single extra feature or ensemble member.
expected_effect: Can capture undercut/traffic pressure and peer-relative context that handcrafted mean/max lap features miss.
risk: More engineering and higher overfit risk than trees; gains may depend on careful regularization and causal masking only to current lap.
